name: pool_visibility_sweep
method: Shallow news-visibility triage of the ENTIRE remaining candidate pool below tier 3 -- all 1,343 companies, 34 batches, grouped web searches (4 names/query). Three-way verdict: NOTABLE (investment/expansion news found), VISIBLE (real public footprint, no expansion news), DARK (no meaningful public footprint). Shallow by design: verdicts are triage labels, not verification -- tier-1/2/3-style deep verification remains the standard for anything promoted out of this layer.
headline: 42% of India's live corporate industrial EC-filer pool is DARK -- 570 of 1,343 companies with paid-up, live clearance filings have NO meaningful public footprint at all; for them the clearance register is the only investment intelligence in existence. 332 (25%) are NOTABLE with expansion news the lead layers had not yet captured; 73 are foreign-linked. Dark rates are remarkably uniform across policy programs (40-46% for chemicals/steel/ethanol) -- opacity is a structural feature of the industrial base, not a sector quirk.
